steps.title: Recognize the negation paired with the actual case
steps.content: The blank comes between “didn't write in Modern English” and “he wrote in Middle English”—a structure that contrasts what is false with what is true.

steps.title: Select the answer that signals this contrast
steps.content:
The logical relationship is contradiction/substitution: the first clause states what Chaucer did NOT do; the blank introduces the corrective replacement of what he DID do instead.
  • Choice A — "for this reason,": signals cause-effect, implying the Middle English writing was caused by not writing in Modern English. No causal logic exists here. ✗
  • Choice B — "instead,": signals substitution — replacing the negated claim with the actual fact. Fits the "didn't X; instead, Y" structure. ✓
  • Choice C — "furthermore,": signals addition of a new equal-weight point, but there's nothing being added — the second clause corrects the first. ✗
  • Choice D — "likewise,": signals similarity/parallel, but the two clauses are opposites (Modern vs. Middle English), not parallels. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
